back: A service letting any developer send prompts to a provider's model over the internet and get responses back, paid per token. How most AI products are built.
Developer's app -> provider's API -> model -> response -> back to the app, which displays, stores, or acts on it.
A fixed pipeline of steps where an LLM handles one or a few stations. The model never chooses the next step.
Workflow = recipe (fixed steps, predictable). Agent = chef (given a goal, improvises the steps). Known path -> workflow; open-ended -> agent.
Email -> LLM extracts invoice -> save to database -> reply -> notify Slack. Five steps, one of them AI, no agent needed.
Predictable, testable per step, cheaper (fewer model calls), and easy to debug. Most corporate 'AI' is a workflow.
Model (via API) + prompts + tools + RAG + memory, arranged as either a workflow or an agent loop. Every AI product is some arrangement of these.
